non-0 sets to log frequency scale

ELSE/spectrograph~.body: [spectrograph~] is an abstraction for visualizing FFT amplitudes from 0Hz to Nyquist. It uses a Hann table for the analysis.

title: speed
ELSE/speed.description: change bpm speed over amount of beats

messages.description: <f,f> — target, number of beats; <f,f,f> — start, target, number of beats

methods.description: floats/lists precede by 'set' waits for a bang to trigger
triggers the object after a 'set' message

ELSE/speed.body: [speed] is somewhat related to [line]. You give it a target bpm value and an amount of time in beats to reach it (speeding up or down). Then, the overall tempo in ms is output (via the right outlet) and bpm values are given for each beat until it reaches the target (left outlet), which correctly emulates an accelerando or ritardando.

title: spread

messages.description: output to an outlet as the result of the comparison

flags.name: -mode <f>
flags.description:
sets mode, 0 and 1 (in reverse to [moses])
default: 0

ELSE/spread.body: Similar to [moses], [spread] compares an incoming float to the arguments and spreads to different outputs. One difference to [moses] is that it takes multiple arguments and has an extra mode.

title: spread.mc~
ELSE/spread.mc~.description: spread signal input to a multichannel output
ELSE/spread.mc~.body: [spread.mc~] spreads any number of input channels across any number of output channels with equal power panning. It takes and outputs a multichannel signal.

title: spread~
ELSE/spread~.body: [spread~] spreads any number of input channels across any number of output channels with equal power panning.

title: square~

flags.name: -midi
flags.description: sets frequency input in MIDI pitch

flags.name: -mc <list>
flags.description: sets multichannel output with a list of frequencies

methods.description: nonzero sets frequency input in MIDI pitch
<channel, freq> set a single frequency channel
nonzero sets to soft sync mode

ELSE/square~.body: [square~] is a square oscillator that accepts negative frequencies, has inlets for pulse width, phase sync and phase modulation.

title: sr~

messages.description: get sample rate frequency or period

flags.name: -khz
flags.description: sets output to frequency in kHz

flags.name: -sec
flags.description: sets output to period in seconds

methods.description: set and get the sample rate frequency in Hz
set and get the sample rate frequency in kHz
set and get the sample rate period in ms
set and get the sample rate period in seconds

ELSE/sr~.body: [sr~] can set the sample rate and also reports the sample rate frequency or period when loading the patch, when receiving a bang or when it changes. The frequency is reported either in Hz or kHz and the period either in seconds os milliseconds. Unlike [samplerate~], it doesn't report up/down sampling rates. The default output is the sample rate frequency in Hz.

title: stack
ELSE/stack.description: stack messages and output via fifo/lifo

methods.description: outputs all messages and clears them
outputs number of elements in right outlet

ELSE/stack.body: [stack] stores messages as stacks, where it outputs and removes them one by one when receiving bangs. An argument defines the output order, which can be 'fifo' (first in first out) or 'lifo' (last in first out) - This needs to be set before storing data.

title: standard~

messages.description: 2 floats set x[n-1] and y[n-1], respectively

title: status

messages.description: resends bang according to current status
if a zero to non-0 transition is detected
if a non-0 to zero transition is detected

ELSE/status.body: [status] sends a bang in the left outlet for "zero to non-0" transitions and a bang in the right outlet for "non-0 to zero" transitions.

title: status~

messages.description: impulse if a zero to non-0 transition is detected
impulse if a non-0 to zero transition is detected

ELSE/status~.body: [status~] is a signal version of [status]. It sends an impulse in the left outlet for "zero to non-0" transitions and an impulse in the right outlet for "non-0 to zero" transitions.

title: stepnoise

messages.description: frequency in Hz up to 100 (negative values accepted)
Step noise in the range from 0 - 127

methods.description: a float sets seed, no float sets a unique internal

ELSE/stepnoise.body: [stepoise] is a control rate stepnoise. It doesn't need the DSP on to function. Give it a seed value if you want a reproducible output.

title: stepnoise~

flags.name: -mc <list>
flags.description: sets multichannel output with a list of frequencies

methods.description: a float sets seed, no float sets a unique internal
<channel, freq> set a single density channel

ELSE/stepnoise~.body:
[stepnoise~] is a low frequency (band-limited) noise generator without interpolation; therefore, it holds at a random value, resulting in random steps. Random values are between -1 and 1 at a rate in Hz (negative frequencies accepted). Use the seed message if you want a reproducible output.
The [stepnoise~] object produces frequencies limited to a band from 0 up to the frequency it is running. It can go up to the sample rate, and when that happens, it's basically a white noise generator.

title: stereo.rev~
ELSE/stereo.rev~.body: [mono.rev~] is a stereo input/output reverb abstraction, a variant of [mono.rev~] with two independent reverb channels.

title: store

messages.description: outputs element from sequence and goes forward
sets index and outputs stored messages at index
messages to be stored in a sequence
bang at the end of sequence or 'n' number of messages

arguments.description: float - non-0 sets to store elements with the patch

methods.description: sets index to output on next bang message
outputs number of elements in the right outlet
clicking on the object opens [store]'s window

ELSE/store.body: [store] is an abstraction based on [text] that stores incoming messages sequentially.

title: stream
ELSE/stream.body: [stream] mode makes a list with the last N received items. The N is set as an argument or in the right inlet. A negative N inverts the list.

title: stretch.shift~

messages.description: resets to the beginning of the delay line
the buffer signal of the corresponding channel

ELSE/stretch.shift~.body: [stretch.shift~] is like [gran.player~], but for live input. It provides independent time stretching and pitch shifting via granulation.

title: sum~
ELSE/sum~.description: sum multichannel signals into a single channel

arguments.description: non zero sums, zero passes unchanged

methods.description: non zero sums to a single channel, zero passes unchanged

ELSE/sum~.body: [sum~] takes a multisignal connection and sums them into a single mono channel.

title: susloop~

messages.description: 0 to non-0: starts and loops. Non-0 to 0: loop is off
the control signal for the sampler

methods.description: stops and goes back to 0, expects a trigger to restart
sets sustain loop range in samples <start, end>

ELSE/susloop~.body: [susloop~] generates a control signal designed to read buffers and loop at a given section. It responds to a gate input (float or signal).

title: suspedal

messages.description: the pitch part of a note message
a pair of pitch/velocity values from note messages
the velocity part of a note message
the pitch value of a note message
the velocity value of a note message

arguments.description: float - non-0 turns on the pedal

methods.description: clears the memory (no note-off messages are output)

ELSE/suspedal.body: [suspedal] holds MIDI Note-Off messages when on and sends them out when it's switched off. It has two sustain modes, 'regular' (default) and 'tonal' - as in the design of modern pianos.

title: svfilter~

methods.description: clears the filter in case of a blow-up

ELSE/svfilter~.body: [svfilter~] implements Chamberlin's state-variable filter algorithm, which outputs lowpass, highpass, bandpass, and bandstop simultaneously in parallel (in this order from left to right).

title: swap2

messages.description: any message to be swapped to the right outlet
any message to be swapped to the left outlet

ELSE/swap2.body: [swap2] is like [swap] but swaps any messages, not just floats.

messages.description: symbol to be converted to any message

title: synth~

messages.description: midi note messages, pitch and velocity

arguments.description: (optional) extra arguments sent to the abstraction

flags.name: -mc
flags.description: sets to multichannel output (default single channel)

methods.description: abstraction name and optional number of voices
sets number of voices (1 sets to monophonic)
non zero sets to multichannel output

ELSE/synth~.body: The [synth~] object loads synth abstractions that follow a simple template. It is just a wrapper over [clone] and [mono]/[voices] but may provide some convenience, specially to newbies. One convenience is the ability to open/load different abstractions in runtime. The arguments are abstraction name and number of voices. If no arguments are given, a default and simple abstraction is loaded as a template and basis to build new synths. Clicking on the object opens the loaded abstration. Shift click opens a panel to choose an abstraction.

title: sysrt.in
ELSE/sysrt.in.body: [sysrt.in] detects System and real-time MIDI messages from raw MIDI data.

title: sysrt.out

messages.description: clock, tick, start, continue, sensing, reset

ELSE/sysrt.out.body: [sysrt.out] formats and sends raw MIDI system and real-time messages.

title: tabgen

messages.description: commands to generate functions (details in [pd examples])

flags.name: -ms
flags.description:
set table size to ms
default: points

ELSE/tabgen.body: [tabgen] is an abstraction that generates different functions for a given table name.

title: tabplayer~

messages.description: gate on/impulse — starts, gate off — stops (not in trigger mode)
when it stops/finishes playing or when looping

methods.description: non-0 sets to trigger mode, zero sets to gate mode
sets start and end point range proportionally (from 0 to 1)
sets position proportionally within range (from 0 to 1)
resets range from 0 to array size
start playing, optional <start in ms, end in ms, speed rate>
stops playing and outputs 0 (cannot be resumed)
pauses at a particular point (can be resumed)
sets to crossfade mode when looping
sets sample rate of sample (default, Pd's sample rate)

ELSE/tabplayer~.body: [tabplayer~] plays arrays, it's more powerful than [tabplay~] as it has multichannel support and can play backwards and in different speeds. It can also loop.

title: tabreader

methods.description: sets an entire array to be used as a waveform
non-0 sets index to read from

ELSE/tabreader.body: [tabreader] accepts indexes from 0 to 1 by default and reads an array with different interpolation methods with multi channel support. There's no need to have guard points in the array as these are taken care of internally.

title: tabreader~

methods.description: sets an entire array to be used as a waveform

ELSE/tabreader~.body: [tabreader~] accepts indexes from 0 to 1 by default and reads an array with different interpolation methods with multi channel support. There's no need to have guard points in the array as these are taken care of internally.

title: tabwriter~

messages.description: signal to record into an array channel 0
signal to record into an array channel $nth
non-0 starts recording at that index, 0 stops it
when recording reaches the end of the array

methods.description: end point in ms (default array's initial size)
sets start and end point range proportionally (from 0 to 1)
resets start/end from 0 to (current) array's size
non-0 continue recording from where it last stopped
non-0 enables loop recording, 0 disables it

ELSE/tabwriter~.body: [tabwriter~] records up to 64 signal channels into arrays and can be triggered with sample accuracy.

title: tap

flags.name: -ms
flags.description:
sets output to ms
default: bpm

ELSE/tap.body: The [tap] object detects a tapped tempo by analyzing the time distance between two bangs or the time a gate is opened.

title: tempo

messages.description: tempo in ms, Hz or bpm

flags.name: -ms
flags.description:
sets time measure to ms
default: bpm

flags.name: -hz
flags.description:
sets time measure to Hz
default: bpm

flags.name: -seed <float>
flags.description:
sets seed
default: no float sets a unique internal

methods.description: sets time to ms, optional floats set tempo and swing
sets time to Hz, optional floats set tempo and swing
sets time to bpm, optional floats set tempo and swing
a float sets seed, no float sets a unique internal

ELSE/tempo.body: The [tempo] object sends bang at a time specified in bpm or ms. It has a swing parameter and is turned on and off by a toggle.

title: tempo~

flags.name: -ms
flags.description:
sets time measure to ms
default: bpm

flags.name: -hz
flags.description:
sets time measure to Hz
default: bpm

flags.name: -seed <float>
flags.description:
sets seed
default: no float sets a unique internal

methods.description: sets time to ms, optional floats set tempo and swing
sets time to Hz, optional floats set tempo and swing
sets time to bpm, optional floats set tempo and swing
a float sets seed, no float sets a unique internal

ELSE/tempo~.body: The [tempo~] object is like [imp~], but sends impulses at a time specified in bpm as in a metronome and is turned on/off by a gate input.

title: timed.gate

messages.description: control trigger with the gate value
control trigger with the last/initial gate value

ELSE/timed.gate.body: When receiving a bang or a float, [timed.gate] sends a timed gate (with the value of the float for the given duration, 0 otherwise).

title: timed.gate~

messages.description: impulse trigger with the gate value
control trigger with the gate value
control trigger with the last/initial gate value

ELSE/timed.gate~.body: When receiving an impulse or a float, [timed.gate~] sends a timed gate (with the value of the impulse/float for the given duration, 0 otherwise). The length of the gate is given in ms, if 0 or less is given, no gate is output.

title: toggleff~
ELSE/toggleff~.body: [toggleff~] toggles between 0 and 1 upon receiving a trigger. A trigger happens when the signal changes from non-positive to positive.

title: touch.in

flags.name: -ext
flags.description:
only listen to external input source
default: no

methods.description: non zero sets to listen only to external input source

ELSE/touch.in.body: [touch.in] extracts MIDI aftertouch information from raw MIDI input (such as from [midiin]).

title: touch.out
ELSE/touch.out.body: [touch.out] formats and sends raw MIDI aftertouch messages. An argument sets channel number (the default is 1).

title: tremolo~
ELSE/tremolo~.body: The [tremolo~] abstraction performs amplitude modulation It takes a signal input, a tremolo frequency and a tremolo depth. Classic amplitude modulation is achieved with a depth of 1!

title: trig.delay2~
ELSE/trig.delay2~.body: After receiving a trigger (non-positive to positive transition), [trig.delay2~] sends an impulse delayed by a given time in ms. After receiving a trigger to delay, following triggers received before its delayed output are ignored.

title: trig.delay~
ELSE/trig.delay~.body: After receiving a trigger (non-positive to positive transition), [trig.delay~] sends an impulse delayed by a given time in ms. Any triggers which arrive before the delayed output resets and reschedules the delay, in the same way as the [delay] object.

title: trig2bang

messages.description: when detecting zero to non-0 transitions

ELSE/trig2bang.body: [trig2bang] detects zero to non-0 transitions. This can be used to detect and convert triggers (a gate) to a bang.

title: trig2bang~

messages.description: when detecting zero to non-0 transitions

ELSE/trig2bang~.body: [trig2bang~] detects zero to non-0 transitions. This can be used to detect and convert trigger signals (impulses and gate) to a bang.

title: trighold~

messages.description: clear and set output to zero

ELSE/trighold~.body: [trighold~] holds a trigger value. A trigger happens when a transition from 0 to non-0 occurs.

title: tri~

flags.name: -midi
flags.description: sets frequency input in MIDI pitch

flags.name: -mc <list>
flags.description: sets multichannel output with a list of frequencies

methods.description: nonzero sets frequency input in MIDI pitch
<channel, freq> set a single frequency channel
nonzero sets to soft sync mode

ELSE/tri~.body: [tri~] is a triangular wave oscillator that accepts negative frequencies, has inlets for phase sync and phase modulation.

title: trunc
ELSE/trunc.body: [trunc] truncates floats and lists towards zero, that means only the integer value is considered, like in vanilla's [int].

title: trunc~
ELSE/trunc~.body: [trunc~] truncates a signal towards zero, that means only the integer value is considered.

title: unite

messages.description: any message to be united/converted into a symbol

ELSE/unite.body: [unite] unites and converts messages into a symbol message.

title: unmerge

messages.description: a message whose elements get unmerged
number of elements in a group
the extra elements of the message
the bits of the unmerged message

arguments.description: number of outlets from 2-512 (default 2), there's an extra outlet for the extra elements

ELSE/unmerge.body: [unmerge] separates the elements of a message into groups of any size (default 1). Each group is sent out a separate outlet, extra elements are sent to an extra rightmost outlet.

title: unmerge~

messages.description: number of channels in a group
channel group of a multichannel signal
extra channel group of a multichannel signal

arguments.description: number of outlets, there's an extra outlet for the extra channels

ELSE/unmerge~.body: [unmerge~] separates the channels of a multichannel signal in groups of any size (default 1). Each group is sent out a separate outlet, extra elements are sent to an extra rightmost outlet. If the number of channels is less than the number of outlets, zeroes are output.

title: var
ELSE/var.body: [var] is similar to [value] but it can set and recall more than one variable as lists.

title: velvet~

flags.name: -seed <float>
flags.description:
sets random seed
default: unique internal

methods.description: a float sets seed, no float sets a unique internal

ELSE/velvet~.body: [velvet~] is a velvet noise generator, which randomly chooses either positive (1) or negative (-1) impulses at random positions in a given period set in Hz. A polarity bias is possible to set the amount of positive and negative impulses. A time regularity parameter forces a impulses with less randomness. An amplitude irregularity forces random values (different than 1 or -1). The object has support for multichannel.

title: vibrato~
ELSE/vibrato~.body: [vibrato~] is a pitch shifter abstraction with an LFO controlling the pitch deviation in cents.

title: vocoder~

messages.description: list of frequencies (in MIDI) for each channel

arguments.description: List of frequency (in MIDI) for each channel
arguments.default: equally dividing the range in MIDI from 28 and 108 for the number of channels

ELSE/vocoder~.body: [vocoder~] is classic cross synthesis channel vocoder abstraction.

title: voices

messages.description: other cutom messages to route to active voices

flags.name: -split
flags.description: split mode allocates outputs the given number of voices via separate outlets

methods.description: sets index offset (in the context of "list" mode)
clears memory and output hanging notes
sets number of voices (in list mode only)

ELSE/voices.body: [voices] is used to implement polyphonic synths. It's like Pd Vanilla's [poly] object, but with more functionalities. By default, it routes the voices to different outlets, but it also has a list mode similar to [poly].

title: voices~

messages.description: MIDI note messages (note and velocity pair or more)

methods.description: sets number of voices (in list mode only)

ELSE/voices~.body: [voices~] is an abstraction based on [voices] and generates multichannel signals that can be used to control pitch and gate with polyphony.

title: vsaw~

flags.name: -midi
flags.description: sets frequency input in MIDI pitch

flags.name: -mc <list>
flags.description: sets multichannel output with a list of frequencies

methods.description: nonzero sets frequency input in MIDI pitch
<channel, freq> set a single frequency channel
nonzero sets to soft sync mode

ELSE/vsaw~.body: [vsaw~] is a variable sawtooth waveform oscillator that also becomes a triangular oscillator. It accepts negative frequencies, has inlets for width, phase sync and phase modulation.

title: vu~

methods.description: sets window and hop size in samples

ELSE/vu~.body: [vu~] combines [rms~] and [peak~] to provide dBFs output to Pd Vanilla's [vu] GUI.

title: wavetable~
wt~

flags.name: -midi
flags.description:
sets frequency input in MIDI pitch
default: Hz

flags.name: -soft

sets to soft sync mode
default: hard

non-0 sets to soft sync mode

ELSE/wavetable~.body: [wavetable~] is an interpolating wavetable oscillator like Pd Vanilla's [tabosc4~]. It accepts negative frequencies, has inlets for phase sync and phase modulation.

title: white~

methods.description: a float sets seed, no float sets a unique internal

ELSE/white~.body: [white~] is a white noise generator from a pseudo random number generator algorithm.

title: wrap2

messages.description: fold or output the last wrapped value (only float)

ELSE/wrap2.body: [wrap2] wraps between a minimum and maximum value.

title: wrap2~
ELSE/wrap2~.body: [wrap2~] wraps between a low and high value.

flags.name: -n <list>

flags.description: sets frequency input in MIDI pitch

non zero sets to soft sync mode

ELSE/wt2d~.body: [wt2d~] is an interpolating wavetable oscillator like [wt~], but besides a horizontal dimension, it can also scan through a vertical dimension of a sliced wavetable. As other oscilltors in ELSE, it has input for phase modulation and sync.

title: xfade.mc~
ELSE/xfade.mc~.body: [xfade.mc~] performs an equal power crossfade between two multichannel sources.

messages.description: left 'n' inlets are channels of input A. middle 'n' inlets are channels of input B

ELSE/xfade~.body: [xfade~] performs an equal power crossfade between two sources that can have 1 to 64 channels each.

title: xgate.mc~

ELSE/xgate.mc~.body: [xgate.mc~] routes to a channel output in a multichannel connection.

ELSE/xgate2.mc~.body: [xgate2.mc~] routes an input signal to 'n' multichannel outlet with equal power crossfade between two adjacent channels.

ELSE/xgate2~.body: [xgate2~] routes an input signal to 'n' specified outlets with equal power crossfade between two adjacent channels.

title: xgate~

ELSE/xgate~.body: [xgate~] routes between multiple outputs with equal power crossfading.

ELSE/xmod2~.body: [xmod2~] performs is a variant of [xmod~], but only performs frequency modulation (so far) and uses a different and cheap sine waveform that promotes more chaotic and cool sounds.

ELSE/xselect.mc~.body: [xselect.mc~] selects between multiple channels from a multichannel connection (maximum 512 channels) with equal power crossfading.

title: xselect2.mc~

methods.description: 1 sets to indexed mode, 0 to non-indexed mode

title: xselect2~

ELSE/xselect2~.body: [xselect2~] selects between multiple inputs with equal power crossfade between two adjacent channels.

title: xselect~

messages.description: reports input number and off status

ELSE/xselect~.body: [xselect~] selects between multiple inputs with equal power crossfading.

title: zbiplot

ELSE/zbiplot.body: [zbiplot] plots the biquad coefficients as poles and zeros on the Z-Plane.

ELSE/zerocross~.body: [zerocross~] sens impulses when crossing from non-positive to positive (left outlet), from positive to non-positive (middle outlet) and both (right outlet).

title: accum

messages.description: sets a new value to be accumulated and outputs it

methods.description: sets a new value to be accumulated

cyclone/accum.body: [accum] accumulates to a value by either adding an increment value to it or multiplying it by a given factor.

title: acos

messages.description: output the arc-cosine of stored value

cyclone/acos.body: [acos] calculates the arc-cosine of a given number.

title: acosh

messages.description: input to hyperbolic arc-cosine function, this value is stored and updates the argument
calculates output according to the stored input
the hyperbolic arc-cosine of the input

cyclone/acosh.body: [acosh] calculates the hyperbolic arc-cosine of a given number.

title: acosh~

messages.description: the hyperbolic arc-cosine of the input

cyclone/acosh~.body: Use [acosh~] to output the hyperbolic arc-cosine of each input sample (probably only useful for mathematical calculations).

title: acos~
cyclone/acos~.body: Use [acos~] to output the arc-cosine of each input sample.

title: active
cyclone/active.body: [active] outputs 1 when a patch canvas becomes active (its title bar is highlighted and it's the front-most window), and outputs 0 when inactive. See examples below.

title: allpass~
cyclone/allpass~.body: Use [allpass~] for filtering and delay effects. The All Pass filter passes all frequencies without altering the gain - but changes the frequencies' phase.

title: anal

methods.description: clears analysis but remembers last input

cyclone/anal.body: [anal] reports how many times it received a number pair. The output is designed to serve as input to [prob] and is composed of: 1) the previously received number, 2) current input number, 3) occurrence (how many times this number pair has been received).

title: append
cyclone/append

messages.description: a message that will be combined to the stored message

arguments.description: sets message to append to the input

methods.description: updates argument (message to append to the input message)

cyclone/append.body: [append] will add messages set as argument to the end of any message sent to the input.

title: asin

messages.description: output the arc-cosine of stored value

cyclone/asin.body: Use [asin~] to output the arc-sine of a given number.

title: asinh

messages.description: input to hyperbolic arc-sine function, this value is stored and updates the argument
calculates output according to the stored input
the hyperbolic arc-sine of the input

cyclone/asinh.body: Use the [asinh] object to calculate and output the hyperbolic arc-sine of any given number.

title: asinh~

messages.description: the hyperbolic arc-sine of the input

cyclone/asinh~.body: Use [asinh~] to output the hyperbolic arc-sine of each input sample (probably only useful for mathematical calculations).

title: asin~
cyclone/asin~.body: Use [asin~] to output the arc-sine of each input sample.

title: atan2~

messages.description: a" value input to an arc-tangent function
b" value input to an arc-tangent function

cyclone/atan2~.body: Use the [atan2~] object to output the arc-tangent of two given values ("a" and "b") calculated as: Arc-tangent (a/b).

title: atanh

messages.description: input to hyperbolic arc-tangent function, this value is stored and updates the argument
calculates output according to the stored input
the hyperbolic arc-tangent of the input

cyclone/atanh.body: use the [atanh] object to calculate the hyperbolic arc-tangent of any given number.

title: atanh~

messages.description: the hyperbolic arc-tangent of the input

cyclone/atanh~.body: Use [atanh~] to output the hyperbolic arc-tangent of each input sample (probably only useful for mathematical calculations).

title: atan~
cyclone/atan~.body: Use [atan~] to output the arc-tangent of each input sample (probably only useful for mathematical calculations).

title: atodb

messages.description: outputs the last converted float value

methods.description: sets the float value to be converted via bang

cyclone/atodb.body: Use [atodb] to convert a linear amplitude value to a deciBel Full Scale (dBFS) equivalent. Negative values convert to -Inf as if the input is "0".

title: atodb~
cyclone/atodb~.description: linear amplitude to dBFS conversion for signals
cyclone/atodb~.body: Use the [atodb~] to convert a signal representing a linear amplitude value to a deciBel Full Scale (dBFS) equivalent.

title: average~

messages.description: number of last samples to apply the average to
the moving average over the last 'n' samples

arguments.description: sets mode: bipolar, absolute, or rms

methods.description: outputs average over positive and negative values

cyclone/average~.body: Use [average~] for a signal running/moving average over the last 'n' given samples. The average is done in 3 modes: bipolar (default), absolute and rms.

title: avg~

messages.description: the signal to take its absolute average
compute average since the last received bang

cyclone/avg~.body: Use the [avg~] object to keep track of the absolute average from the input signal when receiving a bang. The output value is the sum of the absolute values of the input divided by the number of elapsed samples.

title: bangbang

messages.description: a bang or any other message triggers [bangbang]
a bang via each outlet in the right to left order

cyclone/bangbang.body: When it receives any input, [bangbang] outputs bang messages out of each outlet (in the usual right-to-left order). The number of outlets is determined by the argument.

title: bitand~

messages.description: converted to integer and used as bitmask
signal as result of the "Bitwise-AND" operation

arguments.description: converted to integer and used as bitmask

methods.description: bits plus 32 bit (0/1) values sets a raw 32-bits bitmask
<0-3>: modes of conversion to integers (details in help)

cyclone/bitand~.body: [bitand~] compares the bits of two values with "Bitwise-AND" (bits are set to 1 if both are "1", 0 otherwise). It compares two signals or a signal to a given bitmask. It has 4 modes of comparison (see help file).

title: bitnot~
cyclone/bitnot~.body: [bitnot~] takes a signal and performs Bitwise NOT operation, also known as bitwise inversion or one's complement (all bit values of 1 are set to 0 and vice versa). The operation is done in two modes (see help file).

title: bitor~

messages.description: converted to integer and used as bitmask
signal as result of the "Bitwise-OR" operation

arguments.description: converted to integer and used as bitmask

methods.description: bits plus 32 bit (0/1) values sets a raw 32-bits bitmask
<0-3>: modes of conversion to integers (details in help)

cyclone/bitor~.body: [bitor~] compares the bits of two values with "Bitwise OR" (bits are set to 1 if any of them is "1", 0 otherwise). It compares two signals or a signal to a given bitmask. TIt has 4 modes of comparison (see help file).

title: bitsafe~
cyclone/bitsafe~.description: replace NaN/Inf and denormal signals with 0

messages.description: input signals to have NaN/Inf values replaced with 0
the signal which has 0 values where NaN/Inf values existed

cyclone/bitsafe~.body: [bitsafe~] replaces NaN (not a number) and +/- infinity values of an incoming signal with zero, which is useful in conjunction with the bitwise operators in cyclone or any other situation where these values are possible. Also, bitsafe~ in cyclone filters out denormals and turns them to zero, even though the original MAX object doesn't.

title: bitshift~

methods.description: specifies number and direction of shift - positive values shift that number of bits to the left while negative shift to the right

cyclone/bitshift~.body: [bitshift~] takes a signal and performs Bitwise Shifting (shift bit values to the left or right) depending on a shift value (positive values shift that number of bits to the left, while negative values shift to the right). The operation is done in two modes (see help file).

title: bitxor~

messages.description: converted to integer and used as bitmask
signal as result of the "Bitwise-XOR" operation

arguments.description: converted to integer and used as bitmask

methods.description: bits plus 32 bit (0/1) values sets a raw 32-bits bitmask
<0-3>: modes of conversion to integers (details in help)

cyclone/bitxor~.body: [bitxor~] compares the bits of two values with "Bitwise eXclusive OR" (bits are set to 1 if different, 0 otherwise). It compares two signals or a signal to a given bitmask. It has 4 modes of comparison (see below).

title: bondo

messages.description: one element messages go to the corresponding outlet. If there are more than 1 element, they're parsed among the inlets unless the "n" argument is used

arguments.description: ''n' sets outlets to send messages with more than 1 element

cyclone/bondo.body: [bondo] synchronizes and outputs messages when any inlet gets a message. It can wait a time interval for input messages (see [pd sync time]) or send them automatically.

messages.description: resets by sending note-offs and erasing events' memory
voice number from report (when more notes are reported)
velocity of note from report (0 means Note-Off)
event number from note duration report
delta-time (time difference in ms) between Note-Ons

title: bucket
cyclone/bucket.description: pass numbers from outlet to outlet

messages.description: outputs all stored values without shifting

arguments.description: <0> holds the input and passes it on the next round, <1> outputs the inputs immediately

methods.description: sets input value and outputs it to all outlets
suspends output, but continues to shift input internally
resumes the output after being freezed
or 'L2R'/'ltor': sets shift from left to right (default)
or 'R2L'/'rtol': sets shift from right to left
shifts stores values in loop (according to direction)

cyclone/bucket.body: As [bucket] is input with floats, it outputs them shifting from outlet to outlet in a rotational pattern (or "passing the bucket" as in a bucket brigade).

title: buddy

messages.description: any message type to be synced
same as sending a 0 to the inlet

cyclone/buddy.body: [buddy] synchronizes arriving data and outputs them only if messages have been sent to all inlets. The output is in the usual right to left order, and all input is cleared after that.

title: buffer~

arguments.default: 0 or -1 if audio file is given

methods.description: trims the sample to a range in ms and resizes the buffer
sets file to load from (no symbol opens dialog box)
reads and loads the previously opened file again
sets file to write to (no symbol opens dialog box)
sets bit depth - see [pd read/write] for details
sets file to write in wave (no symbol opens dialog box)
sets file to write in aiff (no symbol opens dialog box)
symbol sets file type to write ("wave" or "aiff")
sets sample rate for writing a file
normalizes to the given float value

cyclone/buffer~.body: [buffer~] stores audio in a memory buffer. It read/writes multichannel audio files and can be used in conjunction with [play~] and other related objects. This is an abstraction without the full functionalities from the Max/MSP original.

title: buffir~

messages.description: the signal to be convolved with samples from the buffer

cyclone/buffir~.body: [buffir~] is a table/buffer based FIR (finite impulse response) filter. An input signal is convolved with 'n' samples of a buffer.

title: capture

messages.description: stored items in sequential order (on dump message)
number of items received since last 'count' message

arguments.description: no arg: as decimal, "x": hex, "m": < 128 — decimal / > — hex, "a": only symbols

methods.description: outputs number of received items since last 'count' message
outputs stored elements sequentially (first in first out)
opens editing window, same as clicking on it
saves to a file (no symbol opens dialog box)

cyclone/capture.body: [capture] stores items in the received order for viewing, editing, and saving to a file. If the maximum number of items is exceeded, the earliest stored item is dropped.

title: capture~

messages.description: signal to be stored in a text format

arguments.description: f" - collect data for the specified number of samples
up to 10 number indices within a signal block. If no list is given, entire block is stored

methods.description: clear stored values and starts capturing again
save to file (no symbol opens dialog box)

cyclone/capture~.body: [capture~] is designed for signal debugging/investigation, it captures samples into a text window or text file.

title: cartopol

messages.description: converts the last received coordinates pair
real part from the cartesian coordinates
imaginary part from the cartesian coordinates
phase in radians (-pi to pi) of the polar form

cyclone/cartopol.body: [cartopol~] converts cartesian coordinates (real / imaginary) to polar coordinates (amplitude / phase).

title: cartopol~

messages.description: real part of the complex signal in cartesian form
imaginary part of the complex signal in cartesian form
amplitude output of the polar form
phase in radians (-pi to pi) output of the polar form

cyclone/cartopol~.body: Use the [cartopol~] object to convert signal values representing cartesian coordinates to a signal composed of polar coordinates - useful for spectral processing.

title: change~

messages.description: when DSP is off, sets an internal value to be compared with incoming signal when DSP is on
1 when increasing, -1 when decreasing and 0 for no change

cyclone/change~.body: Use [change~] to detect if signal is changing and its direction. It outputs "1" if the signal is increasing, "-1" if it's decreasing and "0" if it doesn't change.

title: click~
cyclone/click~.body: [click~] generates an impulse when receiving a bang. An impulse is a single sample with a value of one followed by zeros, and this is the default impulse generated by [click~], but you can can also an impulse with a different amplitude value or even as a list of values (for band limited impulses - maximum is 256 sample values).

title: cyclone/clip
cyclone/clip.description: constrain values to a given range
cyclone/clip.body: Use [clip] to constrain values from floats or lists between a range specified by a minimum and a maximum value.

title: cyclone/clip~
cyclone/clip~.description: constrain a signal to a given range
cyclone/clip~.body: Use the [clip~]* object to constrain input signals between two specified values (minimum and maximum). If the maximum value is less than the minimum, it becomes the minimum value and vice-versa.

1st element is an int address to store remaining data to

integer or symbol address of the stored message

non-0 prevents from searching for a file

<1> save contents with the patch, <0> don't save
default: 0

<1> saves contents with the patch, <0> doesn't

the address, an element (starts at 1) and any value to substitute it to
opens the window with the data collection (allows manual data editing)

int or symbol address and any message to be stored at it
same as nsub, but the substituted message is sent out

closes the data window containing the collection
saves file specified by the symbol (without a symbol, a dialog window opens)
resaves last file - if no file had been saved, a dialog box is shown

cyclone/coll.body: [coll] stores/edits any messages at given addresses (an integer or a symbol). If an input list starts with an int, it stores the other element(s) at that int address.

title: comb~

messages.description: signal to pass through comb filter

cyclone/comb~.body: [comb~] is a comb filter, use it for filtering and delay effects.

title: cyclone_comment

flags.name: @fontname <symbol>
flags.description: default 'dejavu sans mono' or 'menlo' for mac

methods.description: appends a message to the comment
prepends a message to the comment
sets RGB text color (values from 0 to 255)
sets RGB background color (values from 0 to 255)

cyclone/comment.body: [comment] is a GUI meant to be only a comment (a label or a note) that can be typed directly into it when in Edit mode. It is widely used in cyclone's documentation and (unlike pd vanilla's comments) allows you to set font, size, color, background color, bold, italic, underline and justification. This object is not being fully compliant to Max6+ versions! It is also now related to [else/note].

title: cosh

messages.description: input to hyperbolic cosine function, this value is stored and updates the argument
calculates output according to the stored input
the hyperbolic cosine of the input

cyclone/cosh.body: [cosh] calculates the hyperbolic cosine of a given number.

title: cosh~
cyclone/cosh~.body: Use [cosh~] to output the hyperbolic cosine function of each input sample (probably only useful for mathematical calculations).

title: cosx~
cyclone/cosx~.description: signal cosine function with radian input
cyclone/cosx~.body: Properly designed for mathematical operations, [cosx~] it expects an input in radians to calculate the cosine of each input sample.

title: counter

messages.description: goes to the next value and outputs it
sets counter to minimum value (no output)

(in any inlet) prints current settings state on the Pd window

title: count~

messages.description: triggers counter and start counting from minimum
triggers counter and start counting from the given number
up to 4 floats that resets the arguments
0 to non-0 transition starts counter. Non-0 to 0 stops it
sets maximum value to loop at (0 eliminates the maximum)
outputs minimum value or count values when triggered

arguments.description: specify if it's initially off <0> or on <1>

methods.description: stops the counter and resets to minimum
sets minimum value without triggering the counter
sets minimum and maximum values without triggering

cyclone/count~.body: [count~] outputs a signal increasing by 1 for each sample elapsed. It starts at a given minimum and it can loop at a given maximum value (which is actually that value - 1).

title: cross~
cyclone/cross~.body: [cross~] is a of 3rd order butterworth crossover filter.

title: curve~

messages.description: up to 42 triplets composed of: 1) destination value, 2) time (ms) & 3) curve factor (from -1 to 1)
jumps immediately to that value (unless the duration is set before to other than 0 in the mid inlet)
curve duration in ms - works only if a float is sent into the left inlet after, and it works only once
current value or a ramp towards the target
a bang is sent when final target from last ramp is reached

cyclone/curve~.body: Similar to [line~], but [curve~] produces curved (non linear) ramp signals. Below, when receiving 2 triples (of destination, time & curve factor), [curve~] generates a simple Attack-Release envelope.

title: cycle

messages.description: any message type. Messages with more than one element outputs each element to a different outlet
a float or a symbol atom element, depending on the input

arguments.description: mode: Non-0 sets to "event sensitive mode

methods.description: sets the next output outlet (starting at 0)

cyclone/cycle.body: Each incoming number is sent to the next outlet, wrapping around to the first outlet after the last has been reached, completing a cycle in a "round-robin scheduling" way.

title: cycle~

messages.description: sets frequency in Hz (negative values allowed)

arguments.description: sets array used as the wavetable (but only reads 512 points from the buffer)
sets offset within the given array

methods.description: only a "set" message sets to the default cosine wave
sets array name and offset (reads only 512 points)
sets an array using its entire length
sets array (analogous to the setall message)
changes the buffer size to powers of 2 (16 to 65536)

cyclone/cycle~.body: [cycle~] is a linear interpolating oscillator* that reads repeatedly through one cycle of a waveform. The default internal waveform is one cycle of a cosine wave (16k in size, 64 bits), but you can set other waveforms from given arrays.

title: dbtoa

messages.description: outputs the last converted float value

methods.description: sets next float value to be converted via bang

cyclone/dbtoa.body: Use [dbtoa] to convert dB values to corresponding linear amplitude.

title: dbtoa~
cyclone/dbtoa~.description: convert dBFS to linear amplitude for signals
cyclone/dbtoa~.body: [atodb~] takes any given signal representing a dBFS amplitude value and outputs a signal which is a linear amplitude conversion of the input.

title: decide

messages.description: sets the seed for the random number generator. 0 will use a random seed, any other integer float is the seed

cyclone/decide.body: [decide] alternates randomly from 1 to 0 (the sequence depends on the seed value).

title: decode
cyclone/decode.description: switch an outlet according to input

messages.description: a number to search for a matching outlet

cyclone/decode.body: [decode] receives a number and looks for a corresponding outlet (numbered from left to right, starting at 0) to switch it on (output: 1) and the others off (output: 0).

title: degrade~

messages.description: signal to be degraded (resampled and quantized)

cyclone/degrade~.body: [degrade~] takes any given signal and reduces the sampling rate and bit-depth as specified/desired.

title: delay~

arguments.description: sets the maximum delay in samples
sets the initial delay in samples

methods.description: sets a new maximum delay size in samples
sets a ramp time to a new target delay value in ms

cyclone/delay~.body: [delay~] delays a signal by a number of samples (thus the delay time depends on the sample rate).

title: deltaclip~

messages.description: any signal whose changes will be clipped
input signal with its change limited by the delta minimum and maximum values

cyclone/deltaclip~.body: [deltaclip~] limits the change between samples in an incoming signal. This is also known as 'slew limiting'. It has a negative maximum delta for when the signal decays and a positive maximum delta for when it rises. When they're both 0, the signal doesn't shift. Below we divide by the sample rate to get the max amplitude shift per second instead of per sample.

title: delta~

messages.description: difference between the current and last sample

cyclone/delta~.body: [delta~] outputs the difference between each incoming sample and the previous sample. So, if the input signal contains 1, 0.5, 2, 0.5, the output would be 1, -0.5, 1.5, -1.5

title: downsamp~

messages.description: rate (in samples) used to downsample the input signal

cyclone/downsamp~.body: [downsamp~] samples and holds a signal received in the left inlet at a rate expressed in samples. No interpolation of the output is performed.

title: drunk

messages.description: sets new current value and outputs it
of floats sets <value, maximum, step size> in this order
random number output as result of the drunken walk

methods.description: seeds the internal random number generator

cyclone/drunk.body: [drunk] generates random numbers within a given step range from the current number (generating a "drunk walk"). The random number is from 0 to a given maximum and differs from the previous number by a random value less than the given step size.

title: edge~
cyclone/edge~.body: [edge~] detects signal transitions from zero to non-0 and vice versa and reports bangs accordingly.

title: equals~
cyclone/==~
cyclone/equals~.description: 1 or 0 (depending on the result of the comparison)

value used for comparison with left inlet's input
1 or 0 (depending on the result of the comparison)

arguments.description: value for comparison with left inlet's input

cyclone/equals~.body: [equals~] or [==~] outputs a signal that is "1" when the left input is equal to the right input/argument or "0" when it isn't.

title: flush

sets the velocity part of the note message
the pitch part of a note message
the velocity part of a note message

methods.description: clears the hanging notes that [flush] keeps track off

cyclone/flush.body: Like a "panic button", [flush] keeps track of Note-on messages that weren't switched off and "flushes" them by sending corresponding note-offs when it receives a bang.

title: forward

messages.description: any message to be sent forward

methods.description: sets an address to send to

cyclone/forward.body: [forward] is similar to [send], but the destination can change with each message (like a semicolon in a message in Pd/Max) - it also sends to anything that a [send] does (like to an array, to "pd", etc..).

title: frameaccum~

arguments.description: ''1' wrap phase between -PI and PI. '0' — accumulate values without bounds

cyclone/frameaccum~.body: [frameaccum~] was specially designed to compute a running phase of FFT frames by keeping an accumulated sum of the values in each sample of an incoming signal block.

title: framedelta~

messages.description: The input on which the deviation will be computed

cyclone/framedelta~.body: The [framedelta~] object was designed specially to compute a running phase deviation of a FFT analysis by subtracting values in each position of its previously received signal block from the current signal block. When used in a FFT subpatch, it can keep a running phase deviation of the FFT because the FFT size is equal to the block size.

title: fromsymbol

just goes right through as a float

a converted message from a symbol

cyclone/fromsymbol.body: [fromsymbol] converts a symbol message to anything (any kind of message). Start by typing a float in the atom symbol box below and see how it converts to a float message, then check more examples to the right.

non-0 value saves the contents of the buffer when the patch is saved
sets the buffer pointer to the specified <float> buffer element
outputs the y value of the buffer element and goes to next pointer
finds the 'x' values which have a 'y' that matches the given number
if a given 'x' doesn't exist, interpolates the 'y' value from 2 neighboring x/y pair
same as interp, but uses the table specified by the symbol <s>
sends the lowest 'y' value in the buffer to the left outlet
sends the highest 'y' value in the buffer to the left outlet
prints status information in Pd main window
reads buffer contents from the file name specified by the symbol. If no symbol is given, a file open box is shown
writes buffer contents to the file name specified by the symbol. If no symbol is given, a file open box is shown

cyclone/funbuff.body: [funbuff] stores, manages and recalls x/y pairs of integers.

title: funnel

messages.description: any data to be tagged and output
sends the last received message in that inlet
an incoming message preceded with the inlet number

methods.description: sets a message and doesn't output it
sets an offset for the inlet numbers

cyclone/funnel.body: [funnel] receives data from many inlets and funnels them to an outlet. The incoming data is tagged with an inlet number to be retrieved with [route] or [spray]. It can also be used to store values into a table/coll.

title: gate

messages.description: reports the open outlet number on the left outlet
message to send through a specified outlet
outlet $nth for routing any received message

cyclone/gate.body: [gate] routes a message from the second inlet to one of 'n' specified outlets or none of them.

title: gate~

messages.description: outlet number to route to - values are truncated to integers and clipped from 0 to number of outlets

cyclone/gate~.body: [gate~] routes an input signal from the second inlet to one of 'n' specified outlets or none of them. If an outlet is not selected, it outputs zero values.

title: grab
cyclone/grab.description: grabs the output of another object

1 or 0 (depending on the result of the comparison)

cyclone/greaterthaneq~.body: [greaterthaneq~] or [>=~] outputs a 1 signal when the left input is greater-than or equal-to the right input or argument and a 0 when it is less-than the right input or argument.

cyclone/>~
cyclone/greaterthan~.description: 1 or 0 (depending on the result of the comparison)

title: histo

input integer values to be stored (from 0 to 'size - 1')

cyclone/histo.body: [histo] records how many times it received a positive integer (no floats allowed). An input number is sent out the left outlet and its occurrence at the right outlet. A number in the right inlet checks and outputs that number's histogram.

title: index~

messages.description: signal read from array without interpolation

cyclone/index~.body: [index~] reads an array without interpolation. The input signal specifies the table index. It's like [tabread~] but works with multi channel arrays and index values are rounded instead of being truncated.

title: iter

messages.description: outputs the last received input as sequential elements
according to the input element, in sequential order

cyclone/iter.body: [iter] is similar to [unnpack], it splits a message (to floats/symbols) but outputs them sequentially in the given order.

sets a message to that inlet without output

cyclone/join.body: [join] takes any type of messages (anything, float, symbol, list) in any inlet and combines them all into an output list.

title: kink~

messages.description: phasor signal to be distorted by kinking it

cyclone/kink~.body: Distort [phasor~] with [kink~]. If the phase input times the slope is less than 0.5, the value is output. Otherwise, a complentary slope is used that goes from 0.5 to 1 (creating a bend or a "kink").

title: lessthaneq~
cyclone/<=~
cyclone/lessthaneq~.description: 1 or 0 (depending on the result of the comparison)

messages.description: value is compared to right inlet's or argument
value for comparison with left inlet
1 or 0 (depending on the result of the comparison)

cyclone/lessthaneq~.body: [lessthaneq~] or [<=~] <~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is greater-than or equal-to the right input or argument and a 0 when it is less-than the right input or argument.

cyclone/<~

value for comparison with left inlet
1 or 0 (depending on the result of the comparison)

cyclone/lessthan~.body: [lessthan~] or [<~] <~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is less-than the right input or argument and a 0 when it is greater-than or equal-to the right input or argument.

title: linedrive

messages.description: input value, to be mapped to output range

cyclone/linedrive.body: [linedrive] scales numbers from one range to another with an exponential curve. First argument is the input range (127 means from -127 to 127), second is output range (1 is from -1 to 1), third is exponential factor and fourth is the time (to be used with line~).

title: line~
cyclone/line~
cyclone/line~.description: a bang is sent when final target is reached

messages.description: jumps immediately to that value unless duration is set to other than 0 via the second inlet
pairs that specify target value and duration (in ms) to reach it (maximum is 128 target-time pairs). For an odd number of elements, the last element is treated as another pair with 0 ms duration
sets the time for the next float send to the left inlet
current value or a ramp towards the target
a bang is sent when final target is reached

cyclone/line~.body: [line~] generates signal ramps or envelopes. It takes a target and a duration (in ms) values and generates a ramp from its current value to the target value in that period. Target and duration can be set as a list or in different inlets.

messages.description: any list, the elements are indexed and output
the elements preceded with an index number, sequentially

arguments.description: array/table name to be used for lookup
initial end point in the array/table

cyclone/lookup~.body: [lookup~] uses arrays as transfer functions for waveshaping distortion, in which signal input values (from -1 to 1) are mapped to the table's indexes.

title: lores~

arguments.description: resonance: 0 (a little bit sharp) — 1 (sharp and LOUD)

cyclone/lores~.body: [lores~] implements an inexpensive resonant lowpass filter. The first argument or middle inlet sets the cutoff frequency, and the resonance is set by the second argument or the right inlet. Below, we use a LFO to control the cutoff, resulting in a filter sweep effect.

title: match

messages.description: one or more elements to look for a match
a message whose elements were matched

arguments.description: elements to match ('nn' = wildcard for any number)

methods.description: clears and sets new elements to match to

cyclone/match.body: [match] sends data when the input matches the arguments.

title: mtr

messages.description: any message to be recorded in that inlet/track
track number & duration (when receiving the `next` message)
recorded messages from the corresponding inlet/track

flags.name: @trackspeed <float>
flags.description: sets speed for all tracks (1 is originally speed, 2 twice as fast, etc). Same if set as a message to the left inlet but in other inlets set individual track speeds

flags.name: @speed <float>
flags.description: sets multiplier to the speed of all tracks, which allows adjust globally on top of individual track speeds. This only works as a message on the left inlet

flags.name: @loop <float>
flags.description: Turns looping on (1) or off (0) for all tracks. Same if set as a message to the left inlet but in other inlets set individual track looping

flags.name: @embed <float>
flags.description: When set to 1, recorded data for all tracks is saved with the patch. Note that recording, changing, or clearing data does not cause the patch to be dirtied.

methods.description: in left inlet: clears all tracks. If followed track numbers, clears those track(s). In other inlets: clears corresponding track
in left inlet: sets a delay value in ms for all tracks to start playing. In other inlets: sets a delay time to that track only

in left inlet: waits a given time in ms after a play message is received before playing back. Unlike delay, first does not alter the delta time value of the first event in a track, it just waits a certain time (in addition to the first delta time) before playing back from the beginning. In other inlets: waits only corresponding track
in left inlet: mutes all tracks while still playing. If followed by track numbers, it mutes those track(s). In other inlets: mutes corresponding track

in left inlet: causes each track to output only the next message in its recorded sequence (the track number and the delta time of each message being output are sent out the leftmost outlet as a list). If followed track numbers, outputs the next message stored in those tracks. In other inlets: outputs the next message stored on corresponding track

in left inlet: plays recorded tracks out the corresponding outlets in the same rhythm/speed as recorded. If followed by track numbers, it plays those tracks. In other inlets: plays corresponding track
in left inlet: opens a previously saved file with the symbol name (or opens a dialog box if no symbol is given). In other inlets: opens a file containing only the track that corresponds to the inlet
in left inlet: begins recording all messages received in the other inlets. If followed by track numbers, it begins recording those tracks. In other inlets: begins recording corresponding track

in left inlet: goes to start of recorded sequence. Can be used when using the 'next' message. If [mtr] is playing or recording, a stop message should precede it. If followed by track numbers, it rewinds those tracks. In other inlets: rewinds corresponding track
in left inlet: stops all recording/playing tracks. If followed by track numbers, it stops those tracks. In other inlets: stops corresponding track
in left inlet: Unmutesd all muted tracks. If followed by track numbers, it unmutes those tracks. In other inlets: unmute corresponding track
in left inlet: saves a file with the symbol name (or opens a dialog box if no symbol is given). In other inlets: saves corresponding track

cyclone/mtr.body: [mtr] records any messages in different tracks and plays them back. Each track records what comes into its inlet and plays it back through the outlet directly below.

title: next
